Just now

Digital marketing in 2026 is evolving at an unprecedented pace. Artificial intelligence, privacy-first strategies, changing search behavior, and increasingly personalized customer experiences are transforming how businesses connect with their audiences. While new tools and technologies continue to emerge, one principle remains constant: brands that provide genuine value, earn trust, and adapt to change will outperform those chasing quick wins.
Drawing inspiration from modern digital marketing best practices, these Eric Porat digital marketing tips highlight the strategies businesses can use to drive sustainable growth, increase visibility, and generate high-quality leads in 2026.
1. Embrace AI While Keeping Content Human
Artificial intelligence has become one of the most powerful tools available to marketers. It can streamline workflows, uncover insights, and improve campaign performance, but it should enhance human creativity — not replace it.
Use AI to:

• Generate content ideas

• Analyze customer behavior and trends

• Optimize advertising campaigns

• Automate repetitive marketing tasks

• Improve workflow efficiency
However, every piece of content should reflect human expertise, original thinking, and real-world experience. Authenticity builds trust, and trust remains one of the strongest competitive advantages in digital marketing.
2. Create Content That Solves Real Problems
Today’s search engines — and AI-powered answer engines — reward content that genuinely helps users.
Rather than publishing generic articles filled with keywords, focus on creating resources that answer important questions and provide practical solutions.
High-performing content includes:

• Step-by-step tutorials

• Industry case studies

• Expert insights

• Frequently asked questions

• Real customer success stories

• Actionable guides
Helpful content not only earns better search rankings but also builds credibility and encourages repeat visitors.
3. Optimize for Both AI Search and Traditional SEO
Search has expanded far beyond Google. Consumers now discover businesses through AI assistants, voice search, video platforms, and social media search.
A successful SEO strategy in 2026 should include:

• Strong E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness)

• Clear, structured headings

• Direct answers to common questions

• Internal linking

• Entity optimization

• High-quality backlinks

• Schema markup where appropriate
Many businesses are also investing in Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) to improve visibility in AI-generated search results alongside traditional search engines.
4. Build Brand Authority and Trust
Customers are far more likely to engage with brands they recognize and trust.
Strengthen your authority by:

• Publishing expert-led content

• Collecting genuine customer reviews

• Earning mentions on reputable websites

• Maintaining active social media profiles

• Sharing customer success stories

• Participating in industry discussions
Brand authority now influences both customer purchasing decisions and search engine visibility.
5. Prioritize First-Party Data
As privacy regulations continue to evolve and third-party cookies become less effective, first-party data has become one of the most valuable marketing assets.
Build your own customer data through:

• Email newsletter subscriptions

• Website registrations

• Customer surveys

• Loyalty and rewards programs

• CRM systems

• Interactive lead generation campaigns
Owning accurate customer data enables better personalization while respecting user privacy.
6. Invest in Video Marketing
Video continues to dominate digital engagement across nearly every platform.
Create content for:

• YouTube Shorts

• Instagram Reels

• TikTok

• LinkedIn

• Facebook
Educational videos, behind-the-scenes content, product demonstrations, customer testimonials, and quick tips consistently outperform purely promotional content. Businesses that publish video regularly often see higher engagement and stronger brand awareness.
7. Personalize Every Customer Experience
Consumers now expect brands to deliver relevant and personalized experiences throughout their buying journey.
Improve personalization by using:

• Dynamic email campaigns

• AI-powered recommendations

• Personalized landing pages

• Audience segmentation

• Behavioral targeting

• Customized offers and messaging
Relevant experiences increase engagement, improve customer satisfaction, and boost conversion rates.
8. Diversify Your Traffic Sources
Relying on a single traffic source is increasingly risky. Search algorithms and platform updates can significantly impact visibility overnight.
A well-balanced marketing strategy should generate traffic from multiple channels, including:

• Organic search

• Email marketing

• Social media

• YouTube

• Referral websites

• Online communities

• Podcasts

• Strategic partnerships
Traffic diversification creates long-term stability and reduces dependence on any one platform.
9. Measure the Metrics That Matter
Not every marketing metric reflects business success. Instead of focusing on vanity metrics such as impressions or likes alone, prioritize performance indicators that directly impact revenue.
Track KPIs such as:

• Conversion rate

• Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)

• Return on Ad Spend (ROAS)

• Customer Lifetime Value (LTV)

• Organic traffic growth

• Qualified leads

• Revenue generated from marketing
Data-driven decisions lead to smarter investments and more consistent growth.
10. Never Stop Testing and Improving
The best marketers treat every campaign as an opportunity to learn.
Continuously test:

• Headlines

• Landing pages

• Email subject lines

• Call-to-action buttons

• Ad creatives

• Pricing strategies

• Offer variations
Even small improvements in conversion rates can produce substantial revenue growth over time. Continuous optimization is what separates successful marketers from average ones.
Final Thoughts
According to Eric Porat, digital marketing success in 2026 is no longer about chasing algorithms or relying on shortcuts. Sustainable growth comes from combining AI-powered efficiency with authentic human expertise, valuable content, trusted brand authority, and data-driven decision-making.
Businesses that invest in high-quality content, embrace AI responsibly, build strong customer relationships, optimize for both traditional and AI-powered search, and continually refine their strategies will be well-positioned for long-term success.
By implementing these proven digital marketing practices, organizations can strengthen their online presence, improve search visibility, attract more qualified leads, and remain competitive in an increasingly dynamic digital landscape throughout 2026 and beyond.
Eric Porat
Entrepreneur | SEO Expert | Digital Marketing Strategist | Investor
